output:
# solution.py
"""Team Collaboration Manager.

A thread-safe, in-memory collaboration platform that manages users,
projects, tasks, messaging, performance dashboards, and exportable
reports.  The public surface (classes, enums, exceptions and the
``TeamCollaborationManager`` facade) is exercised by ``test_solution.py``.

Standard library only.
"""

import copy
import csv
import enum
import io
import threading
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime
from typing import Dict, List, Optional


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Enumerations
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TaskStatus(enum.Enum):
    """Lifecycle states a task can be in."""

    NOT_STARTED = "not_started"
    IN_PROGRESS = "in_progress"
    COMPLETED = "completed"


class UserRole(enum.Enum):
    """Roles a user can hold inside the platform."""

    MEMBER = "member"
    ADMIN = "admin"


class ReportFormat(enum.Enum):
    """Supported export formats for reports."""

    CSV = "csv"
    PDF = "pdf"


class ReportType(enum.Enum):
    """Kinds of reports the manager can build."""

    TEAM_PERFORMANCE = "team_performance"
    PROJECT_PROGRESS = "project_progress"
    INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE = "individual_performance"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Exception hierarchy
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TeamCollaborationError(Exception):
    """Base class for all domain errors raised by the manager."""


class ValidationError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when input fails validation."""


class NotFoundError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when a referenced entity does not exist."""


class AuthorizationError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when an actor is not permitted to perform an action."""


class DuplicateError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when a uniqueness constraint is violated."""


class ConflictError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when an operation conflicts with existing state."""


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Data models
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
@dataclass
class Attachment:
    """A file attached to a message."""

    filename: str
    size: int
    uploaded_by: int
    uploaded_at: datetime


@dataclass
class User:
    """A registered platform user."""

    id: int
    username: str
    email: str
    role: UserRole = UserRole.MEMBER
    created_at: Optional[datetime] = None


@dataclass
class Project:
    """A project that groups tasks and conversations."""

    id: int
    name: str
    start_date: datetime
    end_date: datetime
    owner_id: int
    description: str = ""
    created_at: Optional[datetime] = None


@dataclass
class Task:
    """A unit of work belonging to a project."""

    id: int
    project_id: int
    title: str
    status: TaskStatus = TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED
    assignee_id: Optional[int] = None
    deadline: Optional[datetime] = None
    started_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    completed_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    created_at: Optional[datetime] = None


@dataclass
class Message:
    """A message posted against a project or a task."""

    id: int
    author_id: int
    body: str
    project_id: Optional[int] = None
    task_id: Optional[int] = None
    parent_id: Optional[int] = None
    attachments: List[Attachment] = field(default_factory=list)
    created_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    edited_at: Optional[datetime] = None


@dataclass
class Rating:
    """A peer rating left for a user."""

    id: int
    rater_id: int
    ratee_id: int
    score: int
    feedback: str = ""
    task_id: Optional[int] = None
    created_at: Optional[datetime] = None


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Internal collection wrapper
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class _Collection:
    """A thin id -> entity map that shares a single lock with its siblings.

    The collections deliberately share one lock so that operations spanning
    several collections (for example deleting a project and cascading to its
    tasks and messages) remain atomic.
    """

    def __init__(self, lock):
        self._lock = lock
        self.items = {}


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Manager facade
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TeamCollaborationManager:
    """Coordinates users, projects, tasks, messaging and reporting.

    All public mutating and reading operations are guarded by a single
    re-entrant lock so the manager is safe to use from multiple threads.
    Entities returned to callers are deep copies, preventing external
    mutation from leaking back into internal state.
    """

    def __init__(self, now=None):
        self._now = now if now is not None else datetime.now
        self._lock = threading.RLock()

        # Sibling collections share the same lock instance.
        self.users = _Collection(self._lock)
        self.projects = _Collection(self._lock)
        self.tasks = _Collection(self._lock)
        self.messages = _Collection(self._lock)
        self.performance = _Collection(self._lock)  # holds ratings
        self.reports = _Collection(self._lock)       # stateless, lock holder

        # Secondary indexes.
        self._tasks_by_project = {}    # project_id -> list[int]
        self._tasks_by_assignee = {}   # user_id -> set[int]
        self._messages_by_project = {}  # project_id -> list[int]
        self._messages_by_task = {}     # task_id -> list[int]
        self._ratings_by_ratee = {}     # user_id -> list[int]
        self._username_to_id = {}
        self._email_to_id = {}

        self._id_counter = 0

    # -- id helper (caller must hold the lock) ----------------------------
    def _next_id(self):
        self._id_counter += 1
        return self._id_counter

    # -- internal getters (caller must hold the lock) ---------------------
    def _require_user(self, user_id):
        user = self.users.items.get(user_id)
        if user is None:
            raise NotFoundError("user {0} not found".format(user_id))
        return user

    def _require_project(self, project_id):
        project = self.projects.items.get(project_id)
        if project is None:
            raise NotFoundError("project {0} not found".format(project_id))
        return project

    def _require_task(self, task_id):
        task = self.tasks.items.get(task_id)
        if task is None:
            raise NotFoundError("task {0} not found".format(task_id))
        return task

    def _require_message(self, message_id):
        message = self.messages.items.get(message_id)
        if message is None:
            raise NotFoundError("message {0} not found".format(message_id))
        return message

    # -- validation helpers ----------------------------------------------
    @staticmethod
    def _validate_non_empty(value, field_name):
        if not isinstance(value, str) or not value.strip():
            raise ValidationError("{0} must be a non-empty string".format(field_name))
        return value.strip()

    @staticmethod
    def _validate_email(email):
        if not isinstance(email, str):
            raise ValidationError("email must be a string")
        cleaned = email.strip()
        if not cleaned or "@" not in cleaned:
            raise ValidationError("email must contain '@'")
        return cleaned

    # ====================================================================
    # User management
    # ====================================================================
    def register_user(self, username, email, role=UserRole.MEMBER):
        with self._lock:
            username = self._validate_non_empty(username, "username")
            email = self._validate_email(email)
            if not isinstance(role, UserRole):
                raise ValidationError("role must be a UserRole")
            if username in self._username_to_id:
                raise DuplicateError("username already in use")
            if email in self._email_to_id:
                raise DuplicateError("email already in use")

            user = User(
                id=self._next_id(),
                username=username,
                email=email,
                role=role,
                created_at=self._now(),
            )
            self.users.items[user.id] = user
            self._username_to_id[username] = user.id
            self._email_to_id[email] = user.id
            return copy.deepcopy(user)

    def get_user(self, user_id):
        with self._lock:
            return copy.deepcopy(self._require_user(user_id))

    def list_users(self):
        with self._lock:
            return [
                copy.deepcopy(self.users.items[uid])
                for uid in sorted(self.users.items)
            ]

    def update_user(self, user_id, username=None, email=None, role=None):
        with self._lock:
            user = self._require_user(user_id)

            if username is not None:
                username = self._validate_non_empty(username, "username")
                existing = self._username_to_id.get(username)
                if existing is not None and existing != user_id:
                    raise DuplicateError("username already in use")

            if email is not None:
                email = self._validate_email(email)
                existing = self._email_to_id.get(email)
                if existing is not None and existing != user_id:
                    raise DuplicateError("email already in use")

            if role is not None and not isinstance(role, UserRole):
                raise ValidationError("role must be a UserRole")

            if username is not None and username != user.username:
                self._username_to_id.pop(user.username, None)
                self._username_to_id[username] = user_id
                user.username = username

            if email is not None and email != user.email:
                self._email_to_id.pop(user.email, None)
                self._email_to_id[email] = user_id
                user.email = email

            if role is not None:
                user.role = role

            return copy.deepcopy(user)

    def delete_user(self, user_id):
        with self._lock:
            user = self._require_user(user_id)

            for project in self.projects.items.values():
                if project.owner_id == user_id:
                    raise ConflictError("user owns a project")

            for task_id in self._tasks_by_assignee.get(user_id, set()):
                task = self.tasks.items.get(task_id)
                if task is not None and task.status != TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                    raise ConflictError("user has open tasks assigned")

            self.users.items.pop(user_id, None)
            self._username_to_id.pop(user.username, None)
            self._email_to_id.pop(user.email, None)
            self._tasks_by_assignee.pop(user_id, None)
            self._ratings_by_ratee.pop(user_id, None)

    # ====================================================================
    # Project management
    # ====================================================================
    def create_project(self, name, start_date, end_date, owner_id, description=""):
        with self._lock:
            name = self._validate_non_empty(name, "name")
            if end_date < start_date:
                raise ValidationError("end_date must not precede start_date")
            self._require_user(owner_id)

            project = Project(
                id=self._next_id(),
                name=name,
                start_date=start_date,
                end_date=end_date,
                owner_id=owner_id,
                description=description or "",
                created_at=self._now(),
            )
            self.projects.items[project.id] = project
            self._tasks_by_project.setdefault(project.id, [])
            self._messages_by_project.setdefault(project.id, [])
            return copy.deepcopy(project)

    def get_project(self, project_id):
        with self._lock:
            return copy.deepcopy(self._require_project(project_id))

    def list_projects(self):
        with self._lock:
            return [
                copy.deepcopy(self.projects.items[pid])
                for pid in sorted(self.projects.items)
            ]

    def update_project(self, project_id, name=None, start_date=None,
                       end_date=None, description=None):
        with self._lock:
            project = self._require_project(project_id)

            new_name = project.name
            if name is not None:
                new_name = self._validate_non_empty(name, "name")

            new_start = start_date if start_date is not None else project.start_date
            new_end = end_date if end_date is not None else project.end_date
            if new_end < new_start:
                raise ValidationError("end_date must not precede start_date")

            project.name = new_name
            project.start_date = new_start
            project.end_date = new_end
            if description is not None:
                project.description = description
            return copy.deepcopy(project)

    def delete_project(self, project_id):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)

            for task_id in list(self._tasks_by_project.get(project_id, [])):
                task = self.tasks.items.pop(task_id, None)
                if task is not None and task.assignee_id is not None:
                    assigned = self._tasks_by_assignee.get(task.assignee_id)
                    if assigned is not None:
                        assigned.discard(task_id)
                for message_id in list(self._messages_by_task.get(task_id, [])):
                    self.messages.items.pop(message_id, None)
                self._messages_by_task.pop(task_id, None)
            self._tasks_by_project.pop(project_id, None)

            for message_id in list(self._messages_by_project.get(project_id, [])):
                self.messages.items.pop(message_id, None)
            self._messages_by_project.pop(project_id, None)

            self.projects.items.pop(project_id, None)

    # ====================================================================
    # Task management
    # ====================================================================
    def create_task(self, project_id, title, assignee_id=None, deadline=None):
        with self._lock:
            project = self._require_project(project_id)
            title = self._validate_non_empty(title, "title")
            if assignee_id is not None:
                self._require_user(assignee_id)
            if deadline is not None and deadline < project.start_date:
                raise ValidationError("deadline must not precede project start")

            task = Task(
                id=self._next_id(),
                project_id=project_id,
                title=title,
                status=TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED,
                assignee_id=assignee_id,
                deadline=deadline,
                created_at=self._now(),
            )
            self.tasks.items[task.id] = task
            self._tasks_by_project.setdefault(project_id, []).append(task.id)
            if assignee_id is not None:
                self._tasks_by_assignee.setdefault(assignee_id, set()).add(task.id)
            return copy.deepcopy(task)

    def get_task(self, task_id):
        with self._lock:
            return copy.deepcopy(self._require_task(task_id))

    def list_tasks_by_project(self, project_id):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)
            task_ids = sorted(self._tasks_by_project.get(project_id, []))
            return [copy.deepcopy(self.tasks.items[tid]) for tid in task_ids]

    def list_tasks_by_assignee(self, assignee_id):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(assignee_id)
            task_ids = sorted(self._tasks_by_assignee.get(assignee_id, set()))
            return [copy.deepcopy(self.tasks.items[tid]) for tid in task_ids]

    def _move_assignee(self, task, new_assignee_id):
        if task.assignee_id is not None:
            current = self._tasks_by_assignee.get(task.assignee_id)
            if current is not None:
                current.discard(task.id)
        task.assignee_id = new_assignee_id
        if new_assignee_id is not None:
            self._tasks_by_assignee.setdefault(new_assignee_id, set()).add(task.id)

    def assign_task(self, task_id, assignee_id):
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            self._require_user(assignee_id)
            self._move_assignee(task, assignee_id)
            return copy.deepcopy(task)

    def reassign_task(self, task_id, new_assignee_id):
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            self._require_user(new_assignee_id)
            self._move_assignee(task, new_assignee_id)
            return copy.deepcopy(task)

    def unassign_task(self, task_id):
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            self._move_assignee(task, None)
            return copy.deepcopy(task)

    def set_deadline(self, task_id, deadline):
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            if deadline is not None:
                project = self._require_project(task.project_id)
                if deadline < project.start_date:
                    raise ValidationError("deadline must not precede project start")
            task.deadline = deadline
            return copy.deepcopy(task)

    def update_status(self, task_id, status):
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            if not isinstance(status, TaskStatus):
                raise ValidationError("status must be a TaskStatus")

            now = self._now()
            if status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED:
                task.started_at = None
                task.completed_at = None
            elif status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS:
                if task.started_at is None:
                    task.started_at = now
                task.completed_at = None
            elif status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                if task.started_at is None:
                    task.started_at = now
                task.completed_at = now

            task.status = status
            return copy.deepcopy(task)

    # ====================================================================
    # Messaging
    # ====================================================================
    def post_message(self, author_id, body, project_id=None, task_id=None,
                    parent_id=None, attachments=None):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(author_id)

            if (project_id is None) == (task_id is None):
                raise ValidationError(
                    "exactly one of project_id or task_id is required"
                )

            if project_id is not None:
                self._require_project(project_id)
            if task_id is not None:
                self._require_task(task_id)
            if parent_id is not None:
                self._require_message(parent_id)

            body = body or ""
            attachment_specs = list(attachments or [])
            if not body.strip() and not attachment_specs:
                raise ValidationError("message body or attachments required")

            now = self._now()
            built_attachments = [
                Attachment(
                    filename=filename,
                    size=len(data),
                    uploaded_by=author_id,
                    uploaded_at=now,
                )
                for filename, data in attachment_specs
            ]

            message = Message(
                id=self._next_id(),
                author_id=author_id,
                body=body,
                project_id=project_id,
                task_id=task_id,
                parent_id=parent_id,
                attachments=built_attachments,
                created_at=now,
                edited_at=None,
            )
            self.messages.items[message.id] = message
            if project_id is not None:
                self._messages_by_project.setdefault(project_id, []).append(message.id)
            if task_id is not None:
                self._messages_by_task.setdefault(task_id, []).append(message.id)
            return copy.deepcopy(message)

    def edit_message(self, message_id, editor_id, new_body):
        with self._lock:
            message = self._require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != editor_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("only the author may edit a message")
            if not (new_body or "").strip():
                raise ValidationError("message body must not be empty")
            message.body = new_body
            message.edited_at = self._now()
            return copy.deepcopy(message)

    def add_attachment(self, message_id, uploader_id, filename, data):
        with self._lock:
            message = self._require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != uploader_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("only the author may add attachments")
            if not (filename or "").strip():
                raise ValidationError("filename must not be empty")
            message.attachments.append(
                Attachment(
                    filename=filename,
                    size=len(data),
                    uploaded_by=uploader_id,
                    uploaded_at=self._now(),
                )
            )
            return copy.deepcopy(message)

    def list_messages_by_project(self, project_id):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)
            message_ids = sorted(self._messages_by_project.get(project_id, []))
            return [copy.deepcopy(self.messages.items[mid]) for mid in message_ids]

    def list_messages_by_task(self, task_id):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_task(task_id)
            message_ids = sorted(self._messages_by_task.get(task_id, []))
            return [copy.deepcopy(self.messages.items[mid]) for mid in message_ids]

    def delete_message(self, message_id, requester_id):
        with self._lock:
            message = self._require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != requester_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("only the author may delete a message")
            self.messages.items.pop(message_id, None)
            if message.project_id is not None:
                refs = self._messages_by_project.get(message.project_id)
                if refs is not None and message_id in refs:
                    refs.remove(message_id)
            if message.task_id is not None:
                refs = self._messages_by_task.get(message.task_id)
                if refs is not None and message_id in refs:
                    refs.remove(message_id)

    # ====================================================================
    # Performance dashboard
    # ====================================================================
    def _assignee_tasks(self, user_id):
        task_ids = self._tasks_by_assignee.get(user_id, set())
        return [self.tasks.items[tid] for tid in task_ids if tid in self.tasks.items]

    def get_completion_rate(self, user_id):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            tasks = self._assignee_tasks(user_id)
            if not tasks:
                return 0.0
            completed = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
            )
            return completed / len(tasks)

    def get_average_completion_time(self, user_id):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            durations = self._completion_durations(self._assignee_tasks(user_id))
            if not durations:
                return None
            return sum(durations) / len(durations)

    @staticmethod
    def _completion_durations(tasks):
        durations = []
        for task in tasks:
            if (
                task.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
                and task.started_at is not None
                and task.completed_at is not None
            ):
                durations.append((task.completed_at - task.started_at).total_seconds())
        return durations

    def submit_rating(self, rater_id, ratee_id, score, feedback="", task_id=None):
        with self._lock:
            if rater_id == ratee_id:
                raise ConflictError("users cannot rate themselves")
            if not isinstance(score, int) or isinstance(score, bool):
                raise ValidationError("score must be an integer")
            if score < 1 or score > 5:
                raise ValidationError("score must be between 1 and 5")
            self._require_user(rater_id)
            self._require_user(ratee_id)
            if task_id is not None:
                self._require_task(task_id)

            rating = Rating(
                id=self._next_id(),
                rater_id=rater_id,
                ratee_id=ratee_id,
                score=score,
                feedback=feedback or "",
                task_id=task_id,
                created_at=self._now(),
            )
            self.performance.items[rating.id] = rating
            self._ratings_by_ratee.setdefault(ratee_id, []).append(rating.id)
            return copy.deepcopy(rating)

    def _ratee_ratings(self, user_id):
        rating_ids = self._ratings_by_ratee.get(user_id, [])
        return [
            self.performance.items[rid]
            for rid in rating_ids
            if rid in self.performance.items
        ]

    def get_ratings_summary(self, user_id):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            ratings = self._ratee_ratings(user_id)
            count = len(ratings)
            average = (
                sum(r.score for r in ratings) / count if count else 0.0
            )
            return {
                "count": count,
                "average": average,
                "ratings": [copy.deepcopy(r) for r in ratings],
            }

    def get_user_dashboard(self, user_id):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            tasks = self._assignee_tasks(user_id)
            total = len(tasks)
            completed = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
            in_progress = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS
            )
            not_started = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED
            )
            durations = self._completion_durations(tasks)
            avg_time = sum(durations) / len(durations) if durations else None

            ratings = self._ratee_ratings(user_id)
            rating_count = len(ratings)
            rating_average = (
                sum(r.score for r in ratings) / rating_count
                if rating_count
                else 0.0
            )

            return {
                "user_id": user_id,
                "total_tasks": total,
                "completed_tasks": completed,
                "in_progress_tasks": in_progress,
                "not_started_tasks": not_started,
                "completion_rate": (completed / total) if total else 0.0,
                "average_completion_time": avg_time,
                "rating_count": rating_count,
                "rating_average": rating_average,
            }

    # ====================================================================
    # Reports
    # ====================================================================
    def _project_tasks(self, project_id):
        task_ids = self._tasks_by_project.get(project_id, [])
        return [self.tasks.items[tid] for tid in task_ids if tid in self.tasks.items]

    def project_progress_report(self, project_id):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)
            tasks = self._project_tasks(project_id)
            total = len(tasks)

            status_counts = {status.value: 0 for status in TaskStatus}
            completed = 0
            overdue = 0
            now = self._now()
            per_assignee = {}

            for task in tasks:
                status_counts[task.status.value] += 1
                if task.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                    completed += 1
                if (
                    task.deadline is not None
                    and task.status != TaskStatus.COMPLETED
                    and task.deadline < now
                ):
                    overdue += 1
                if task.assignee_id is not None:
                    bucket = per_assignee.setdefault(
                        task.assignee_id,
                        {"total": 0, "completed": 0, "completion_rate": 0.0},
                    )
                    bucket["total"] += 1
                    if task.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                        bucket["completed"] += 1

            for bucket in per_assignee.values():
                bucket["completion_rate"] = (
                    bucket["completed"] / bucket["total"]
                    if bucket["total"]
                    else 0.0
                )

            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.PROJECT_PROGRESS.value,
                "project_id": project_id,
                "total_tasks": total,
                "status_counts": status_counts,
                "completion_rate": (completed / total) if total else 0.0,
                "overdue_tasks": overdue,
                "per_assignee": per_assignee,
            }

    def _performance_row(self, user_id, tasks):
        total = len(tasks)
        completed = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
        durations = self._completion_durations(tasks)
        avg_time = sum(durations) / len(durations) if durations else None
        return {
            "user_id": user_id,
            "total_tasks": total,
            "completed_tasks": completed,
            "completion_rate": (completed / total) if total else 0.0,
            "average_completion_time": avg_time,
        }

    def team_performance_report(self, project_id=None):
        with self._lock:
            if project_id is not None:
                self._require_project(project_id)
                tasks = self._project_tasks(project_id)
            else:
                tasks = list(self.tasks.items.values())

            grouped = {}
            for task in tasks:
                if task.assignee_id is not None:
                    grouped.setdefault(task.assignee_id, []).append(task)

            rows = [
                self._performance_row(uid, grouped[uid])
                for uid in sorted(grouped)
            ]
            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.TEAM_PERFORMANCE.value,
                "project_id": project_id,
                "rows": rows,
            }

    def individual_performance_report(self, user_id):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            tasks = sorted(self._assignee_tasks(user_id), key=lambda t: t.id)
            total = len(tasks)
            completed = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
            in_progress = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS
            )
            durations = self._completion_durations(tasks)
            avg_time = sum(durations) / len(durations) if durations else None

            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE.value,
                "user_id": user_id,
                "total_tasks": total,
                "completed_tasks": completed,
                "in_progress_tasks": in_progress,
                "completion_rate": (completed / total) if total else 0.0,
                "average_completion_time": avg_time,
                "tasks": [copy.deepcopy(t) for t in tasks],
            }

    # -- export -----------------------------------------------------------
    def export_report(self, report, fmt):
        if not isinstance(fmt, ReportFormat):
            raise ValidationError("fmt must be a ReportFormat")
        if not isinstance(report, dict):
            raise ValidationError("report must be a report dictionary")

        if fmt == ReportFormat.CSV:
            return self._export_csv(report)
        if fmt == ReportFormat.PDF:
            return self._export_pdf(report)
        raise ValidationError("unsupported report format")

    def _export_csv(self, report):
        out = io.StringIO()
        writer = csv.writer(out)
        report_type = report.get("report_type")

        if report_type == ReportType.TEAM_PERFORMANCE.value:
            writer.writerow(
                [
                    "user_id",
                    "total_tasks",
                    "completed_tasks",
                    "completion_rate",
                    "average_completion_time",
                ]
            )
            for row in report.get("rows", []):
                writer.writerow(
                    [
                        row.get("user_id"),
                        row.get("total_tasks"),
                        row.get("completed_tasks"),
                        row.get("completion_rate"),
                        row.get("average_completion_time"),
                    ]
                )
        elif report_type == ReportType.PROJECT_PROGRESS.value:
            writer.writerow(["metric", "value"])
            writer.writerow(["project_id", report.get("project_id")])
            writer.writerow(["total_tasks", report.get("total_tasks")])
            writer.writerow(["completion_rate", report.get("completion_rate")])
            writer.writerow(["overdue_tasks", report.get("overdue_tasks")])
            for status_value, count in report.get("status_counts", {}).items():
                writer.writerow(["status_" + str(status_value), count])
        elif report_type == ReportType.INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE.value:
            writer.writerow(["task_id", "title", "status"])
            for task in report.get("tasks", []):
                status = getattr(task, "status", None)
                writer.writerow(
                    [
                        getattr(task, "id", ""),
                        getattr(task, "title", ""),
                        status.value if isinstance(status, TaskStatus) else "",
                    ]
                )
        else:
            keys = list(report.keys())
            writer.writerow(keys)
            writer.writerow([report[k] for k in keys])

        return out.getvalue()

    def _export_pdf(self, report):
        return _build_pdf(self._report_to_lines(report))

    @staticmethod
    def _report_to_lines(report):
        lines = ["Report: {0}".format(report.get("report_type", "report"))]
        for key, value in report.items():
            if key == "report_type":
                continue
            if isinstance(value, (list, dict)):
                lines.append("{0}: {1} entries".format(key, len(value)))
            else:
                lines.append("{0}: {1}".format(key, value))
        return lines


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Minimal PDF writer
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
def _build_pdf(lines):
    """Build a small but structurally valid single-page PDF document."""

    def _escape(text):
        return (
            text.replace("\\", "\\\\")
            .replace("(", "\\(")
            .replace(")", "\\)")
        )

    content_parts = ["BT", "/F1 12 Tf", "50 760 Td", "14 TL"]
    for index, line in enumerate(lines or [""]):
        if index == 0:
            content_parts.append("({0}) Tj".format(_escape(str(line))))
        else:
            content_parts.append("T*")
            content_parts.append("({0}) Tj".format(_escape(str(line))))
    content_parts.append("ET")
    content = "\n".join(content_parts).encode("latin-1", "replace")

    objects = [
        b"<< /Type /Catalog /Pages 2 0 R >>",
        b"<< /Type /Pages /Kids [3 0 R] /Count 1 >>",
        b"<< /Type /Page /Parent 2 0 R /MediaBox [0 0 612 792] "
        b"/Contents 4 0 R /Resources << /Font << /F1 5 0 R >> >> >>",
        b"<< /Length "
        + str(len(content)).encode("ascii")
        + b" >>\nstream\n"
        + content
        + b"\nendstream",
        b"<< /Type /Font /Subtype /Type1 /BaseFont /Helvetica >>",
    ]

    pdf = bytearray(b"%PDF-1.4\n")
    offsets = []
    for number, body in enumerate(objects, start=1):
        offsets.append(len(pdf))
        pdf += "{0} 0 obj\n".format(number).encode("ascii")
        pdf += body
        pdf += b"\nendobj\n"

    xref_position = len(pdf)
    count = len(objects) + 1
    pdf += b"xref\n"
    pdf += "0 {0}\n".format(count).encode("ascii")
    pdf += b"0000000000 65535 f \n"
    for offset in offsets:
        pdf += "{0:010d} 00000 n \n".format(offset).encode("ascii")
    pdf += b"trailer\n"
    pdf += "<< /Size {0} /Root 1 0 R >>\n".format(count).encode("ascii")
    pdf += b"startxref\n"
    pdf += "{0}\n".format(xref_position).encode("ascii")
    pdf += b"%%EOF"
    return bytes(pdf)
